title: Smoky Australian Beef Skewers
description: Sour, tender, nostalgic Aussie dish featuring extra‑virgin olive oil, quick sauté, and a smoky kick—perfect for a sugar‑free‑friendly barbecue.

ingredients.name: Bamboo skewers, soaked in water for 30 min

instructions: Pat the beef cubes dry with paper towels; this helps the oil to coat and the spices to cling.
In a shallow dish, combine olive oil, salt, pepper, smoked paprika, cumin, garlic powder, lemon zest, and lemon juice; whisk until a uniform rub forms.
Toss the beef cubes in the rub, ensuring each piece is well coated; let marinate for 10 minutes.
Thread 4–5 beef cubes onto each soaked bamboo skewer, leaving a little space between them for even heat distribution.
Preheat a grill or cast‑iron skillet over medium‑high heat; the temperature should reach about 200 °C (400 °F).
Place the skewers on the grill and sear 2–3 minutes per side, turning once, until the exterior is charred and the internal temp reaches 55 °C (130 °F) for medium‑rare.
Remove the skewers and let them rest for 3 minutes; this allows the juices to redistribute.
Plate the skewers and sprinkle with fresh parsley; a squeeze of extra lemon juice brightens the flavor.
Serve immediately with a side of fresh salad or a squeeze of lime for extra zing.
